channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Why Walz’s proposed social media tax is a bad idea
item.description:
It would run afoul of the First Amendment, among other issues. By Annette Meeks Guest contributor to the Minnesota Star Tribune April 20, 2026 ••• What do progressives do when they run out of other people’s money to spend? You don’t have to look any further than Minnesota to find out: Recently Gov. Tim Walz […]

The post Why Walz’s proposed social media tax is a bad idea appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Strengthening Her Family, Building Her Community: Shaneika Murphy’s Passion for Helping Others Flourish
item.description:
After completing the Raising Highly Capable Kids workshop as a parent, Shaneika Murphy now supports other parents as a program facilitator. Her sons volunteer with the program, too. Together, the Murphys are a shining example of how strong families build strong communities.

The post Strengthening Her Family, Building Her Community: Shaneika Murphy’s Passion for Helping Others Flourish appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Raising Highly Capable Kids Program Increases Positive Values and Social Skills in Children
item.description:
GCO and its partners offered 15 RHCK workshops at 12 locations in 2025. Participants took pre- and post-workshop surveys that illustrated the ways families had grown stronger throughout the program.

The post Raising Highly Capable Kids Program Increases Positive Values and Social Skills in Children appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Empowerms] Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service
item.description:
Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service Mississippi Secretary of State Michael Watson returns to the Empower Podcast as he launches his campaign for Lt. Governor. In this conversation, Grant sits down with Watson for a more personal look at the experiences and convictions that have shaped his leadership. From growing up on the Gulf…

The post Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] The NJEA Is Putting Politics Before Kids, Again
item.description:
New Jersey should opt into the Education Freedom Tax Credit. The union’s opposition tells you everything about whose interests it […]

The post The NJEA Is Putting Politics Before Kids, Again first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Sales Tax Modernization Could Help Nebraska Stay Competitive
item.description:
In an increasingly competitive environment, states are competing every day for workers, entrepreneurs, investment, and new residents. Tax policy plays a major role in those decisions. States with more competitive tax structures are often better positioned to attract business investment, encourage job creation, and retain families looking for opportunity.  For Nebraska, that matters. Neighboring states...

The post Sales Tax Modernization Could Help Nebraska Stay Competitive appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF Petitions US Supreme Court to Protect Free Speech
item.description:
Thanks to our friends at the Liberty Justice Center the Rio Grande Foundation is appealing for certiorari (an appeal for our case to be heard) against New Mexico Secretary of State Maggie Toulouse Oliver. The case is called: Rio Grande Foundation v. Oliver. The case is based on the proposition that Americans have a constitutional […]

The post RGF Petitions US Supreme Court to Protect Free Speech appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] 2026 Legislative Wrap-Up
item.description:
As the 132nd Maine Legislature raced toward adjournment, lawmakers pushed through a wave of major bills in just a matter of days. The final stretch of the session was fast-moving, and decisions were made that will shape Maine’s economy, healthcare system, and tax climate for years to come. Maine Policy Institute closely tracked many of […]

The post 2026 Legislative Wrap-Up appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF in Las Cruces Sun News: Las Cruces overspent. It’s time to cut | Opinion
item.description:
The following appeared in the Las Cruces Sun-News on April 26, 2026. After years of overspending the City of Las Cruces, like Albuquerque, faces a budget deficit.  Unlike Albuquerque which has lost people, Las Cruces continues to grow and has seen a 7.08% increase since the 2020 census. That means that the City can and should be able to […]

The post RGF in Las Cruces Sun News: Las Cruces overspent. It’s time to cut | Opinion appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] Coalition forms to educate Arkansans on dangers of Educational Rights Amendment
[Opportunityarkansas] Two groups leading initiatives to end Arkansas’ income tax
[Mspolicy] Investing in the Next Generation of Mississippi Leaders
item.description:
In April, we had the opportunity to welcome students from Pearl River County High School’s Student Council to our office during their visit to Jackson. As part of a broader trip to tour the State Capitol and meet with legislators, the group made time to sit down with Douglas Carswell and our team to learn […]

The post Investing in the Next Generation of Mississippi Leaders appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] The Business of Hate: Why Alabama and the Nation Must Reject the SPLC’s Extremism
item.description:
For decades, the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC) has operated from the Poverty Palace in Montgomery, casting itself as the moral arbiter of American life.

The post The Business of Hate: Why Alabama and the Nation Must Reject the SPLC’s Extremism appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Two labor law updates for the 21st century
item.description:
Remote work, flex time and independent contracting have reshaped the American workforce, creating new opportunities for both employers and employees. But in some states, including New Hampshire, outdated labor laws create obstacles to innovation, lost opportunities for employees and unnecessary costs for employers.  To modernize New Hampshire labor laws, legislators are addressing two outdated statutory […]

The post Two labor law updates for the 21st century appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Gambling Breeds Greed and Corruption
item.description:
Campaign money from the sports betting industry is pouring into Alabama ahead of the May 19 primary.

The post Gambling Breeds Greed and Corruption appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: James Briggs: The New Journalism WE DON’T ENJOY discouraging a fellow journalist. First of all, nobody cares. It is “inside baseball,” as an editor of mine liked to say. Our members have more important things to do. Still, any job has standards and after some decades in the business (note that I didn’t say “profession”) I have a Read the full article...

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Report: Several Wausau Schools Among Wisconsin’s Very Best
item.description:
For Immediate Release:  May 7, 2026 Contact: Press@ReformingGoverment.org   IRG Report: Several Wausau Schools Among Wisconsin’s Very Best Jefferson Elementary, Newman Catholic recognized for student success   Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) released on May 7 “Wausau Wins: How Wausau Became a Wisconsin Education Leader,” a report highlighting the city’s standout […]

The post IRG Report: Several Wausau Schools Among Wisconsin’s Very Best appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Beacontn] Beacon Poll: May 2026 Results
item.description: Today, we released the latest installment of the Beacon Poll, a statewide survey of 1,200 Tennessee voters featuring questions about voters’ views on energy sources and production in Tennessee, the condition of roads and what revenue sources could be used to improve them, and political approval ratings at the state and federal level.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Behind the curtain, Evers administration diverts taxpayer money to fund environmental bureaucracies
item.description:
The Wisconsin Department of Administration has spent nearly $2 million operating two state offices that have since 2019 been denied funding by the Legislature.

item.title: [Mspolicy] The Recovery of the American Mind: Why Suicidal Empathy Is the Next Conservative Frontier
item.description:
It was a particular pleasure this week to welcome Gad Saad and his wife to Mississippi. Gad is one of the most important conservative thinkers writing today. His new book, Suicidal Empathy: Dying to Be Kind, is published next month, and I am the proud owner of a signed copy. Gad Saad's new book is […]

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut’s Warehouse Mandate Is Getting National Attention — for the Wrong Reasons
item.description:
In March, Connecticut enacted one of the country’s most aggressive warehouse workplace laws, tucked inside an emergency-certified omnibus package rushed through the legislature during a special session.  The U.S. Chamber […]

The post Connecticut’s Warehouse Mandate Is Getting National Attention — for the Wrong Reasons  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Teacher Appreciation Should Not Be Tied to Spending on Education
item.description: This week is Teacher Appreciation Week across the country, and it prompted Governor Brad Little to issue a press release where he had this to say: “This is Teacher Appreciation Week, a meaningful opportunity to recognize the people who stand at the front of our classrooms each day, shaping the future of our state. I […]

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] The Big, Beautiful Bill is law. Now Arkansas has to get it right.
item.description:
Passed almost one year ago, the famous “One Big Beautiful Bill” contains the most transformational welfare reforms in modern history. […]

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] WAOW: Wausau Schools recognized during Teachers Appreciation Week
item.description:
Wausau Schools recognized during Teachers Appreciation Week WAUSAU, Wis. (WAOW) – This week is Teacher Appreciation week, and the non-profit group Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) is applauding Wausau schools. IRG is a non-profit dedicated to making the government better, ensuring kids have good schools to attend. They awarded Newman Catholic, Thomas Jefferson Elementary, and […]

The post WAOW: Wausau Schools recognized during Teachers Appreciation Week appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.description:
The opt-out records in Washington just keep falling as the Freedom Foundation charges deeper into the second quarter.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] DeLauro’s Energy Town Hall Became a Lesson in Coping
item.description:
Congresswoman Rosa DeLauro held a tele-town hall on April 6 to address rising energy costs and soaring gas prices. Connecticut residents called in with concerns about skyrocketing electric bills and […]

item.title: [Empowerms] Josh McManus: Jackson’s Comeback Story
item.description:
Josh McManus: Jackson’s Comeback Story What does it take for a struggling city to turn the corner? In this episode of The Empower Podcast, Grant Callen sits down with urban strategist and entrepreneur Josh McManus to discuss the rise, decline, and revitalization of American cities, and why he believes Jackson still has enormous potential. Drawing…

The post Josh McManus: Jackson’s Comeback Story first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Dictionary Policing: What Omaha’s “Censored Shop” Tells Us About State Cartels
item.description:
If you’re in Omaha’s Blackstone District and you’re looking for a good cocktail, you might stumble into a tucked-away speakeasy now called the “Censored Shop.” Until recently, it went by “Barber Shop Blackstone.” According to the Nebraska Examiner, that name wasn’t a trick—it was a nod to the owners’ dad, who spent his life as...

The post Dictionary Policing: What Omaha’s “Censored Shop” Tells Us About State Cartels appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] 2026 K-12 Fact Book
item.description: Spending, taxpayer aid, student achievement and other data for every public school district in Kansas.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] The Myth of the Moral Vacuum: Why Every Law is a Moral Choice
item.description:
When the debate over vice laws, those governing drugs, gambling, or pornography, reaches the halls of our legislature, a familiar, hollow cry rings out: You can’t legislate morality! While it might be a catchy slogan (oft repeated by the vice peddlers themselves), it is designed to end a conversation before it begins. It suggests that […]

The post The Myth of the Moral Vacuum: Why Every Law is a Moral Choice appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] Venti-Sized Subsidy
item.description: Tennessee taxpayers are about to spend millions on a Starbucks order they didn’t ask for. In April, the world’s largest coffee chain announced it would be opening a Southeast corporate office in Music City.

Florida faces growing pressure to secure healthcare data from foreign cyber threats. Protecting our personal data from falling...

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Billionaires Are Not The Problem
item.description:
“You can’t earn a billion dollars. You just can’t earn that. You can get market power. You can break rules. You can do all sorts of things. You can abuse labor laws. You can pay people less than what they’re worth. But you can’t earn that.” These comments made by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez on a […]

The post Billionaires Are Not The Problem appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Play for the Team on Your Jersey: It’s an Integrity Thing.
item.description: Imagine your favorite sports team. They all wear the same uniform, stand under the same banner, and operate under the same creed — not just to play, but to win according to a shared playbook. Each player brings unique strengths, but when they join the team, they all commit to the same rules, values, and […]

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Big federal bucks so far produce a paltry 21 EV charging stations in Wisconsin
item.description:
Millions of dollars have been spent on the aim to build 80 charging stations for electric vehicles at gas stations, hotels, and other venues across Wisconsin.

item.description:
By Naomi Inman It’s election time in Oregon and the Oregonian editorial board...

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Blog: Massachusetts Needs Growth—Not More of the Same
item.description:
Last week, the Massachusetts Taxpayers Foundation (MTF) released a “Bulletin” announcing its opposition to the tax cut ballot initiative. Odd for a taxpayers foundation to oppose a reduction in taxes. Even odder, however, was the Bulletin’s lack of concern about the Commonwealth’s current economic climate: dismal business formation, job losses, and weakening economic growth across the state. 

The post Blog: Massachusetts Needs Growth—Not More of the Same appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Horse Thieves Matter THE SON OF PIONEERS in the Flint Hills of Kansas, my sheriff grandfather was familiar with the story of “the Sedgwick County Anti-Horse-Thieving Association” — or the SCAHTA as it was never known because they were blessedly free of acronyms back then.  Let us just say that it would not have fit into the defund-the-police messaging, Read the full article...

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Governor Ron DeSantis Unveils Statue Honoring “Father of the Constitution” James Madison in Florida’s Madison County
item.description:
MADISON, Fla.—Today, Governor Ron DeSantis unveiled a new statue honoring President James Madison as part of Florida’s continued...

item.description:
Sunshine laws exist for a reason—to ensure governments aren’t operating in the shadows and to hold leaders accountable by allowing..

item.title: [Mspolicy] Great Nations Aren't Destroyed by Enemies. They're Destroyed by Debt.
item.description:
Imagine your family spent more this year than you earned. It wouldn't be comfortable, but you'd manage. Now imagine your family had spent more than it earned every single year since 2001. By now you'd be destitute. Well, that is exactly what the federal government has been doing. For the past 25 years — ever […]

The post Great Nations Aren't Destroyed by Enemies. They're Destroyed by Debt. appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.description: The post U.S. Energy Secretary Calls for More Capitalism in American Electricity Production — Is Louisiana Listening? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Generational Fault Lines: Bronin’s Upset Win Over Larson Exposes Tensions Within Connecticut’s Labor Movement
item.description:
Former Hartford Mayor Luke Bronin surprised many Connecticut political observers by defeating Congressman John Larson for the Democratic convention endorsement in Connecticut’s 1st Congressional District, setting up an August primary […]

The post Generational Fault Lines: Bronin’s Upset Win Over Larson Exposes Tensions Within Connecticut’s Labor Movement  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] School Results Depend on More Than Just Money
item.description:
Iowa’s urban and suburban school districts demonstrate a wide range of per-pupil spending and are evenly split on both sides of the statewide average. The data suggests there is not a strong relationship between higher per-pupil spending and stronger student outcomes, as some of the best-performing districts rank well below the statewide average for per-pupil spending. The findings reinforce the idea that student success depends less on how much money districts spend and more on how effectively local leaders find a way to deliver education to their unique communities.

The post School Results Depend on More Than Just Money appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Jbartlett] How NH can stop subsidizing out-of-state drivers
item.description:
The New Hampshire Turnpike System facilitates tourism and commuting in the Granite State. The three toll roads slash travel times in key corridors, which improves quality of life and helps the state’s economy grow. Turnpike users pay a small fee for the convenience of using these roads vs. more congested and slower local roads, essentially […]

The post How NH can stop subsidizing out-of-state drivers appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Manchester school board elevates educator staffing above all other priorities
item.description:
With Manchester’s school district looking to trim its proposed 2027 budget to stay within the city’s tax cap, the Board of School Committee took the extraordinary step of forbidding the district from achieving a balanced budget by laying off educators. The move effectively elevates raw educator staffing numbers over all other district priorities, even though […]

The post Manchester school board elevates educator staffing above all other priorities appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Louisiana Students Before Pork and Politics
item.description:
Tax season just ended, and Louisianans likely noticed they kept more of their hard-earned income due to pro-growth fiscal reforms that took effect on January 1, 2025. Those reforms delivered income tax cuts to every Louisiana taxpayer and marked an important step toward making the state more competitive for workers, families, and businesses. As the effects of...

The post Louisiana Students Before Pork and Politics appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Beacontn] Beacon Applauds Department of Labor Decision to Withdraw Unlawful Overtime Rule
item.description: Today, the U.S. Department of Labor will formally publish a final rule overturning the Biden administration’s 2024 overtime rule after multiple federal courts vacated the rule nationwide and the department declined to continue defending it on appeal.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Liberty In Action: $750,000 for American Airlines. Courtesy of Taxpayers
item.description:
The public purse is not a private piggy bank for government leaders to dip into to reward their favorite businesses...

The post Liberty In Action: $750,000 for American Airlines. Courtesy of Taxpayers appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF talks to KOAT 7: What ever happened to taxpayers-subsidized Unser Crossing?
item.description:
New Mexico has a long history of spending taxpayer dollars on failed developments that don’t pan out. Going back nearly 20 years to the Unser Crossing development (originally slated for Unser and Central) was one such plan. Of course, RGF loves economic development. We want to see jobs and businesses locate to our state, but […]

The post RGF talks to KOAT 7: What ever happened to taxpayers-subsidized Unser Crossing? appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Franke: Memorial Day 2026
item.description: by Mark Frank Memorial Day is here again and much of our attention will be on those who died in service to their, our, country. As a popular meme says: All gave some. Some gave all. It is those who gave all that focus our memories now, while in November we thank everyone who served. Read the full article...

item.title: [Mackinac] Michigan legalized dental therapists in 2018, but only a few practice
item.description: State officials should pick up the pace of regulatory reform

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Launches Judicial Scorecard, New IRG Court Watch Website
item.description:
For Immediate Release:  May 18, 2026 Contact: Press@ReformingGovernment.org    IRG Launches Judicial Scorecard, New IRG Court Watch Website New tools provide a one-stop resource to track Wisconsin justices, judges and court activity Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) released a new scorecard evaluating Wisconsin Supreme Court justices and a review of the […]

The post IRG Launches Judicial Scorecard, New IRG Court Watch Website appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Unleashing Hope via K–12 Scholarships for Families: Why Rhode Island Should Opt-In to Federal Education Tax Credits
item.description:
Rhode Island students deserve better. The federal Education Freedom Tax Credit (EFTC) empowers working and middle-class families with scholarships for K-12 private schools, charters, homeschooling, tutoring & more at zero cost to RI taxpayers or public schools. The governor can opt-in today for January 2027 launch. Give Ocean State families real choice and brighter futures. Don’t leave millions in education funding on the table.

The post Unleashing Hope via K–12 Scholarships for Families: Why Rhode Island Should Opt-In to Federal Education Tax Credits appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] A Tale of Two Midwestern Cities: What Omaha Can Learn from Des Moines
item.description:
Note: This is the final blog post by Jakob Myers, the Platte Institute’s intern for the last year. Jakob has made great contributions to important policy areas for us since coming on board. This blog post is adapted from a research paper he wrote for one of his courses this spring and kicks off a...

The post A Tale of Two Midwestern Cities: What Omaha Can Learn from Des Moines appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] New York’s School Districts Plan To Spend Over $37K Per Student
item.description:
Cover Image Credit: CC-BY-SA-3.0/Matt H. Wade at Wikipedia School districts presenting budgets to voters on Tuesday, May 19, plan to....

The post New York’s School Districts Plan To Spend Over $37K Per Student appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Foropportunity] How Georgia’s Governor Impacts Your Daily Life: Understanding the Role and the Election Process
item.description:
From vetoing bills to deciding the budget, check out five ways Georgia’s governor impacts schools, safety, work, and public programs that our families and communities rely on.

The post How Georgia’s Governor Impacts Your Daily Life: Understanding the Role and the Election Process appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] California Lags Far Behind on Local Government Financial Transparency
item.description:
Taxpayers have a fundamental right to know how their money is being managed. Three conditions define a best-practice state regime for local government financial reporting. First, the state should require all local governments above a meaningful revenue threshold to produce independently audited financial statements annually. Second, the state should enforce a prompt filing deadline with...

The post California Lags Far Behind on Local Government Financial Transparency first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Freedom to Flourish Competes for Growth
item.description:
Iowa has pursued a fiscally conservative, growth-oriented strategy centered on lower taxes, restrained spending, and economic competitiveness under Governor Reynolds’ “Freedom to Flourish” philosophy. Recent reforms have strengthened the state’s fiscal position and improved its ability to compete for residents and investment. For Midwestern states like Iowa, long-term success will depend less on geography and more on affordability, fiscal stability, economic opportunity, and disciplined governance.

The post Freedom to Flourish Competes for Growth appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Soak-the-Rich Politics Will Hit Kansas Main Street
item.description: “Tax the rich” sounds easy until lawmakers define “rich” broadly enough to hit the small business owner, farmer, physician, contractor, manufacturer, and family-owned shop trying to stay open in Kansas. That is the danger. Soak-the-rich politics is sold as a tax on billionaires. In practice, it becomes a tax on everyday people who own assets […]

item.title: [Mackinac] Think your electric bill is high?
item.description: You ain’t seen nothing yet

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Illinoisans can read a budget before it passes for the first time in at least 10 years
item.description:
Bucking the practice of recent years, lawmakers introduced the proposed budget more than three weeks before the May 31 end of the session.

The post Illinoisans can read a budget before it passes for the first time in at least 10 years appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Will Law] USDA Settles WILL Lawsuit, Removes Race-Based Discrimination in ﻿Nationwide Farming Programs
item.description:
WILL has successfully secured a major settlement with the USDA, triggering the end of race- and sex-based programs that harmed American farmers for decades. Following a federal lawsuit filed on behalf of Wisconsin dairy farmer Adam Faust, the USDA settled with WILL and agreed to end discriminatory preferences across three federal farming programs.

The post USDA Settles WILL Lawsuit, Removes Race-Based Discrimination in ﻿Nationwide Farming Programs appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Empirecenter] New York Schools will Spend More and Deliver Less – Again
item.description:
As voters across New York head to the polls to decide their local school budgets, the big question – why....

The post New York Schools will Spend More and Deliver Less – Again appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Future of transit in Nampa to be heard at City Council meeting
item.description: Valley Regional Transit (VRT) and Uber are gearing up to provide a proposal to the Nampa City Council on May 21 regarding the future of transit in the city. Ridership on transit in the Treasure Valley has continually dropped over the last decade.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Country Club Plaza Subsidy Deal Reveals What’s Broken in Kansas City
item.description:
Listen to this article I’ve argued for years that Kansas City’s lavish subsidies distort the market while failing to deliver on economic promises. New reporting from the Kansas City Business […]

The post Country Club Plaza Subsidy Deal Reveals What’s Broken in Kansas City appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Noisy Illiteracy
item.description:
Kids can’t read. I’m not kidding, the latest viral story from The New York Times proves this. The possible cause of the national literacy decline: Is it No Child Left Behind? How about increased classroom screen time? Maybe pandemic-era school closures? Ultimately, the article declined to say that there is a “single reason for the...

The post Noisy Illiteracy first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Sparing no expense: How AFT members unknowingly financed Randi Weingarten’s literary vanity project
item.description:
A Freedom Foundation analysis shows that AFT financed Randi Weingarten's recent book with hundreds of thousands of dollars in members' dues and that a shadowy LLC receiving royalty payments from the union was associated with Weingarten herself.

The post Sparing no expense: How AFT members unknowingly financed Randi Weingarten’s literary vanity project appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Xavier Becerra’s plan to inflate California’s housing prices
item.description:
At a recent debate, Xavier Becerra discussed his plan to make housing “more affordable” in California. But his agenda would do the opposite if enacted. If his policy ideas become law, home prices will soar even higher. Becerra, a former Biden Cabinet member, California attorney general, U.S. House member and state lawmaker, has worked in […]

The post Xavier Becerra’s plan to inflate California’s housing prices appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] The Property Tax: Expensive and Outdated
item.description: When my dog gets hold of one of her toys, she won’t let it go. The harder I try to take it, the harder she holds on. I’m the same way about the revolutionary idea of eliminating property taxes in Idaho. The more you try to talk me out of abolishing these unfair, misguided, and […]

item.title: [Will Law] WILL’s America 250 Student Contest: ﻿“What America Means to Me”
item.description:
WILL announced the launch of its statewide student contest, “What America Means to Me,” celebrating the 250th anniversary of American Independence. The contest invites Wisconsin students in kindergarten through 12th grade to submit an original 30-60 second video reflecting on freedom, America’s founding principles, and the opportunities afforded by living in a free society.

The post WILL’s America 250 Student Contest: ﻿“What America Means to Me” appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Student-Athletes Are Not Employees
item.description:
College athletics is approaching a crossroads. After years of rapid, sweeping change in student-athlete compensation practices that have left a patchwork of state regulation in its wake, some in Congress now propose to reclassify collegiate student‑athletes across the country as employees of their universities. While we sympathize with the desire to provide certainty and uniformity to student-athletes and universities, and although we see the need for some revenue-sharing framework, workplace regulation...

The post Student-Athletes Are Not Employees first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation Report Exposes How AFT Used Member Dues to Fund Randi Weingarten’s Vanity Book Project
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today released a new investigative report detailing how the American Federation of Teachers (AFT) spent hundreds of thousands of dollars in member dues to finance the writing, publication, and promotion of union president Randi Weingarten's book, Why Fascists Fear Teachers.

The post Freedom Foundation Report Exposes How AFT Used Member Dues to Fund Randi Weingarten’s Vanity Book Project appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] KCATA Is Still Paying for the Fare-Free Experiment
item.description:
Listen to this article Even after reinstating fares, the Kansas City Area Transportation Authority (KCATA) is warning of route reductions because the agency says city funding will fall short of […]

The post KCATA Is Still Paying for the Fare-Free Experiment appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] A Bears deal shouldn’t leave property taxpayers on the sidelines
item.description:
Any deal linked to a stadium should benefit everyone.

The post A Bears deal shouldn’t leave property taxpayers on the sidelines appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Hospitals Front and Center for Rising Healthcare Costs
item.description:
In a much-anticipated hearing last month, Congress asked chief executives from several of America’s major hospital systems to account for the astronomical cost of healthcare in this country. Lawmakers were certainly asking the right people. Any honest diagnosis of America’s soaring healthcare costs has to place hospitals front and center. Sprawling health systems have managed […]

The post Hospitals Front and Center for Rising Healthcare Costs appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] SURPLUS REVENUES FUEL INCOME TAX CUTS
item.description: (May 2026) One sentence in an annual state report explains how policymakers have achieved a serious fiscal goal: reduction of the top state individual income tax rate under three governors and a succession of Republican legislatures. “(Arkansas) revenues are expanding because the business cycle’s current state is expansion, not contraction.” Policy Foundation research memo, Nov. 2012. […]

item.title: [Mackinac] Michigan could not be built today
item.description: Citizens need a mindset of entrepreneurship, optimism and growth

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Higher Energy Prices Could Cost Families Over $1,100 in 2026
item.description:
Californians face a $1,518 increase in energy costs – higher than the $1,120 increase faced by the average U.S. family and much higher than $809 increase New York residents will see.[1]The below map illustrates the estimated increase in annual energy expenditures between 2025 and 2026 should the current elevated prices persist for the remainder of […]

The post Higher Energy Prices Could Cost Families Over $1,100 in 2026 appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] 2 ways to improve Illinois spending decisions
item.description:
Lawmakers make costly decisions without understanding the economic impact.

The post 2 ways to improve Illinois spending decisions appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Johnlocke] State Board of Elections proposes changes to recount, voter ID rules
item.description:
This is the first a two-part series on proposed election rules changes. Part two will cover proposed rules for absentee voting and voting sites. The North Carolina State Board of Elections (SBE) has proposed a set of election rules changes. Two of the proposals cover rules for recounts and photo ID. This post will include...

The post State Board of Elections proposes changes to recount, voter ID rules appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Ben Franklin and the “essential Liberty” of taxation
item.description: Franklin stood on the idea that no government could be truly representative if the taxing power (whether in exercise or forbearance) lay wholly elsewhere.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Missed Connections: How Louisiana is Avoiding them through Responsible Broadband Deployment
item.description:
The federal Broadband Equity Access and Deployment Program (BEAD) began in 2021 and has followed a long and winding road, spanning different administrations and approaches to broadband policy. From early in the BEAD approval process, Louisiana led the nation in reaching BEAD milestones. The state’s broadband office, ConnectLA, proved adept at meeting requirements while encouraging...

The post Missed Connections: How Louisiana is Avoiding them through Responsible Broadband Deployment appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Iowa Moved to Reduce Barriers to Rural Health Care
item.description:
Research comparing states with and without Certificate of Need (CON) laws suggests CON requirements can limit competition and reduce access to rural health care services. During the 2026 legislative session, Iowa passed significant CON reforms that reduce regulatory barriers and narrow the number of projects requiring state approval. The reforms should make it easier for providers to expand services, encourage investment in underserved areas, and improve health care access for Iowa patients and communities.

The post Iowa Moved to Reduce Barriers to Rural Health Care appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] NC budget teacher pay deal would raise starting salaries to $48,000 – but ignore more serious problems
item.description:
After months of negotiations, House and Senate leaders claim to have reached a budget compromise that includes an average 8% teacher pay raise — one of the largest proposed increases in recent years. Current teachers would also receive bonuses ranging from $500 to $1,000. This teacher pay raise would supposedly bring starting teacher salaries to...

The post NC budget teacher pay deal would raise starting salaries to $48,000 – but ignore more serious problems appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] The Mecklenburg Declaration: Fact or fiction?
item.description:
Today is the 251st anniversary of the Mecklenburg Declaration of Independence. The document was joyously celebrated last year in Charlotte with heightened pageantry on its 250th birthday, and this year we continue to celebrate those patriots who stood tall in 1775 on the road to the Declaration of Independence. Supposedly, upon hearing of the battles...

The post The Mecklenburg Declaration: Fact or fiction? appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Idaho Parental Choice Tax Credit application window re-opens May 21
item.description: Good news for Idaho families! The Idaho Tax Commission has announced it will reopen the application window for the Idaho Parental Choice Tax Credit Program tomorrow, May 21, at 8 a.m. (MDT).

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Pioneer Institute Study Calls for Attrition Strategy to Rein in Record-High Massachusetts State Workforce
item.description:
BOSTON – The size of the state workforce reached an all-time high in 2025, and a new Pioneer Institute study recommends implementing an attrition program that would reduce the number of positions under the governor’s authority by 5,000 and save taxpayers $1.5 billion over five years by backfilling three of every four positions that are vacated.

The post Pioneer Institute Study Calls for Attrition Strategy to Rein in Record-High Massachusetts State Workforce appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Differentiating Between Capacity and Yield
item.description:
Whether it’s an energy project or a water project, it’s important to avoid conflating capacity with actual production, or yield. With energy projects, that difference is much more certain than with water projects. For example, in 2024, California’s lone remaining nuclear power plant at Diablo Canyon, with an output capacity of 2.4 gigawatts, would have produced...

The post Differentiating Between Capacity and Yield first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Will Law] The Gig Is Up for the 2024 Independent Contractor Rule
item.description: The post The Gig Is Up for the 2024 Independent Contractor Rule appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Oklahoma farmers benefit from an assessment limit
item.description: Perhaps the strongest objection to an assessment limit over the last two years came from the Kansas Farm Bureau, which was concerned that a limit “could potentially reduce property taxes for certain classes of property on certain years when the appraised value of that property exceeds 3%” and prompt a shift in the tax burden. […]

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Ferguson Denies Incentives for Data Center Project
item.description:
Listen to this article Data center headlines have been filling newspapers each and every week. Among the myriad proposed developments across the state, one project in Ferguson stood out. Ferguson […]

The post Ferguson Denies Incentives for Data Center Project appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Remove obsolete regulations from city code
item.description:
May 20, 2026, 9 a.m. Honolulu Hale To: Honolulu City Council Committee on Infrastructure, Transportation & Technology Radiant Cordero, Chair Val A. Okimoto, Vice Chair From: Grassroot Institute of Hawaii Ted Kefalas, Director of Strategic Campaigns RE: Bill 35 (2026) — RELATING TO STREETS, SIDEWALKS, MALLS, AND OTHER PUBLIC PLACES Aloha Chair Cordero, Vice Chair Okimoto […]

The post Remove obsolete regulations from city code appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] The Quantum Frontier: Leading the Next Generation of Computing
item.description:
Executive Summary The next consequential technology conversation is already here, and this time, it is not about artificial intelligence. Quantum technology is a fundamentally different approach to conventional computing that harnesses the behavior of subatomic particles to solve problems that have been, until now, completely impossible for current computers. The implications run in two directions:...

The post The Quantum Frontier: Leading the Next Generation of Computing first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Allow more beekeeping to boost agriculture
item.description:
May 20, 2026, 8:30 a.m. Historic County Building To: Kaua’i County Council Mel Rapozo, Chair KipuKai Kuali’i, Vice Chair From: Grassroot Institute of Hawaii Jonathan Helton, Policy Analyst RE: Bill 2997 — RELATING TO APIARIES AND BEEKEEPING Aloha Chair Rapozo, Vice Chair Kuali’i and other members of the Council, The Grassroot Institute of Hawaii supports […]

The post Allow more beekeeping to boost agriculture appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Allow more ADUs to boost housing supply and comply with state law
item.description:
May 20, 2026, 9 a.m. Kalana O Maui Building To: Maui County Council House and Land Use Committee Nohelani U‘u-Hodgins, Chair Kauanoe Batangan, Vice Chair From: Jonathan Helton, Policy Analyst Grassroot Institute of Hawaii RE: Bill 78 (2026) — ON ACCESSORY DWELLINGS ON RESIDENTIALLY ZONED LOTS Aloha Chair U‘u-Hodgins, Vice Chair Bantangan and other members […]

The post Allow more ADUs to boost housing supply and comply with state law appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Is a bachelor’s degree worth the extra debt?
item.description:
The Minnesota Office of Higher Education’s latest student debt report shows that students who earned certificates and associate degrees from the state’s technical and community colleges completed school with significantly lower debt than bachelor’s degree graduates. American Experiment has long argued that the cultural push toward a four-year degree, while the right path for a...

The post Is a bachelor’s degree worth the extra debt? appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] US Army veteran tragically killed thwarting an auto theft
item.description:
Minneapolis’s auto theft problem reached a new depths this week with the death of Amos Ferrier, 38. Ferrier was seriously injured on Friday afternoon, May 16, when he attempted to prevent two suspects from stealing his car, which was parked outside the coffee shop he worked at. When confronted, the suspects abandoned their effort to...

The post US Army veteran tragically killed thwarting an auto theft appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Minneapolis Public Schools, facing a $50 million deficit, plans to build new $105 million Anishinabe Academy school
item.description:
Minneapolis Public Schools, the state’s third largest district, is continuing plans to build a brand new $105 million school to rehome Anishinabe Academy. The PK-5 school “is dedicated to serving and educating the families of Minneapolis through an American Indian, Indigenous lens, with a Native-centered curriculum, and through Ojibwe and Dakota language.” Of the school’s 219 students,...

The post Minneapolis Public Schools, facing a $50 million deficit, plans to build new $105 million Anishinabe Academy school appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Will Spencer Pratt Realign California?
item.description:
By every reasonable standard of governance, California’s elected politicians have made a mess of the Golden State. Reciting the litany of failures has become so common that it’s hardly worth the trouble. Chaotic, unsafe downtowns. Retail businesses giving up and relocating. Chronic government budget deficits, despite the nation’s highest taxes, set to go higher still....

The post Will Spencer Pratt Realign California? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] TOP ARKANSAS INCOME TAX RATE CUT TO 3.7%
item.description: (May 2026) Arkansas policymakers reduced the top state individual income tax rate from 3.9% to 3.7% in early May, using an estimated $334.4 million projected fiscal surplus in the current 2025-26 fiscal year. Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders called a special session of the state legislature April 30 to consider the tax cut. The session convened […]
